response.justification: Bogdan has a long, varied career and genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, which is a relevant niche. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) for a performance-critical routing data pipeline, and Bogdan's C++ experience is essentially limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2007–2008 and a bachelor's thesis project — nearly two decades ago. His primary stack for the past 15+ years has been TypeScript, Node.js, and D-lang. There is no Rust, no routing or navigation pipeline work, no traffic signal integration, and no experience with routing tile generation or geospatial data at production scale for navigation.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Broad full-stack and polyglot background with Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, and exposure to embedded and systems-level work
concerns: No modern C++ experience — last meaningful C/C++ work was in 2007–2008; Rust is entirely absent from the resume
No routing, navigation guidance, or routing tile pipeline experience; GIS work is data-collection oriented, not navigation or turn-by-turn routing
Primary language for the past 15+ years is TypeScript/Node.js, which is misaligned with the systems-programming focus of this role
